title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
summary: CopilotKit is a framework enabling developers to build agent-native applications with generative UI components. It allows agents to render real UI, share state with React or Next.js frontends, and integrate human-in-the-loop approval workflows as an alternative to bolted-on chat windows in SaaS products.
writeup: This video introduces CopilotKit, a framework for building agent-native applications with generative UI, and positions it against the pattern of bolting a chat window onto a SaaS product. It covers how agents can render real UI components, share state with React or Next.js frontends, and run human-in-the-loop approval workflows. The concrete takeaways: agents drive actual interface elements rather than text replies, frontend state stays synchronized with the agent, and approval steps keep a person in control of agent actions.
